text.tspan: CACHE HIERARCHY: every layer caches its slower neighbor.
Latency drops by 10u20131000x at each tier.
The same architectural principle from CPU to global web.
PATTERNS APPLIED AT EVERY TIER
u2022 cache-aside (lazy fill) u2014 see [page](cache-aside.md)
u2022 read-through / write-through / write-behind u2014 see [page](cache-managed-strategies.md)
u2022 TTL + LRU eviction
u2022 cache invalidation on writes (the hard problem)
u2022 stampede protection ([thundering herd](../res/thundering-herd.md))
u2022 negative caching (cache the 'not found' too)
u2022 cache warming on deploy
DESIGN PRINCIPLES
u2022 hot data fits in higher tier; cold data lives lower
u2022 size of each tier shrinks as latency drops
u2022 hit rate at upper tier determines effective latency
u2022 working set << total set (this is why caches work at all)
THE MATH OF HIERARCHY
effective_latency = hit_rate u00d7 cache_latency + (1 - hit_rate) u00d7 backing_latency
Example: 95% Redis hit (1ms), 5% RDBMS (10ms)
Without cache: 10 ms (7x improvement)
HIT RATE MATTERS MORE THAN CACHE SPEED at high hit rates
